tier_rationale: This hardens the Generator execution wrapper and operational guide. It changes harness automation scripts and documentation, but not product runtime code.
request_summary: Harden scripts/run-generator.mjs based on Claude CLI review before building Validator automation.
goal: Make the Generator wrapper safer for real operation by fixing unsupported model defaults, strengthening permission handling, validating references, separating prompt from handoff input, and preventing same-task concurrent runs.

positive: Use official Claude Code model aliases or explicit model IDs; do not default to an undocumented alias.
Keep permission bypass disabled by default.
Preserve fresh execution and no-resume/no-continue policy.
Keep web access disallowed for Generator.
Record validation results in the task ledger.
negative: Do not modify product runtime code under api/, public/, or wrangler.toml.
Do not expose secrets, credentials, PII, or environment variable values.
Do not implement Validator-A automation in this task.
Do not push or merge until validation and reporting gates pass.

success_criteria: scripts/run-generator.mjs defaults to an official Claude Code model alias or explicit model ID, not `best`.
scripts/run-generator.mjs supports permission-mode fallback or clear Resource Failure handling for unsupported permission modes.
scripts/run-generator.mjs validates refs.spec and refs.ledger paths before execution.
scripts/run-generator.mjs separates system guidance from handoff stdin using Claude CLI supported prompt options when available.
scripts/run-generator.mjs prevents concurrent runs for the same Task ID with a lockfile or equivalent guard.
Git write permissions are not enabled by default; if supported, they require an explicit flag.
docs/guides/generator-pipeline-ko.md documents the hardened behavior and remaining operator responsibilities.
Local syntax, dry-run, bypass-block, encoding, diff, and harness audits pass.

context.relevant_history: TASK-20260428-001 added the initial Generator wrapper. Claude CLI review identified hardening issues around model alias, git permissions, prompt separation, ref existence checks, concurrent runs, and stderr sensitivity.
